Mental Health Assessment: The patient, a 35-year-old male, presents for initial psychiatric evaluation with chief complaints of persistent low mood, anhedonia, and sleep disturbance over the past three months. PHQ-9 score is 16, indicative of moderate-to-severe depression. The patient denies suicidal ideation or psychotic symptoms. Treatment plan includes initiation of sertraline 50mg daily with titration, referral to individual psychotherapy (CBT), and follow-up in four weeks to assess medication tolerability and response.
